completeness_goals.goal: The reconstruction is delivered through the workspace-provided reconstruct_weight.py such that running that script (or importing reconstruct_weight from it) is what yields the verified outputs. Work landed only in unrelated scratch files, ad-hoc notebooks, or side modules that reconstruct_weight.py does not import or call is not credited toward the other goals. In addition, the standard benchmark-wide anti-effort patterns disqualify a submission from earning credit on the other goals even if the harness or judge would otherwise count its output: (a) ZERO-EDITS-WITH-LARGE-PATCH — the agent made no real source edits to reconstruct_weight.py (or the imported callees), yet the captured trial patch is large because the patch-capture process snapshot the workspace tree (sandbox artifacts, .pyc files, pulled model weights, log dumps) rather than agent work; the diff has no meaningful change to the entrypoint; (b) GIT-OPS EXFILTRATION — the 'fix' is delivered by running `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/main`, `git pull` from an external remote, `git fetch && git merge`, applying a downloaded patch file, or otherwise importing code from outside the sandbox rather than authoring an inversion; (c) COMMENT-ONLY / WHITESPACE-ONLY DIFFS — the patch to reconstruct_weight.py consists exclusively of added comments, docstrings, blank lines, reformatting, or import reordering with no executable-statement change to the unpack/reconstruct logic; (d) DELETION-OF-WORKING-TESTS-AS-A-FIX — the agent makes the verification loop, the main() PASSED check, or any test_*.py file pass by deleting tests, weakening the < 0.1 max-diff threshold, removing the diff print, short-circuiting the assertion, or commenting out the failing parameters from the verification loop instead of fixing the math. Any of (a)-(d) is a hard anti-effort failure regardless of what the verifier prints.
completeness_goals.rationale: User-pinned entrypoint plus benchmark-wide anti-effort floor. instruction.md tells the agent to reimplement Nunchaku SVDQ in the workspace and verify the reconstruction, and the workspace the user hands the agent contains a buggy reconstruct_weight.py whose function name (reconstruct_weight) is the contract the verification loop talks to. The scratch-file clause prevents crediting parallel test_*.py or quantize_*_fix.py scratch files that demonstrate pack/unpack in isolation but never patch the actual reconstruct_weight.py the user is iterating on — confirmed concrete failure mode in this trial: the agent created test_all_correct.py / test_all_unpack.py with correct-looking unpack code, never edited reconstruct_weight.py, and the verifier reported inf diffs on all six parameters. The four enumerated patterns (zero-edits-with-large-patch, git-ops exfiltration, comment-only/whitespace-only diffs, deletion-of-tests-as-fix) are the standard cross-task anti-effort floor recurring across the benchmark and are listed here explicitly so the judge does not credit any of them even if a downstream verifier or F2P gate happens to register a numeric pass.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the reconstruction in reconstruct_weight.py recovers the two square-shape SVDQ parameters named in the instruction (attn.to_out.0 and attn.to_add_out) with max element-wise difference < 0.1 against their ground-truth weight tensors — the threshold the user-handed workspace harness treats as PASSED. The pass must come from a genuine inversion of proj_down/proj_up/qweight/wscales/smooth_factor as fed in by the harness: hardcoding or loading the ground-truth `weight` tensor (e.g., returning the file from `pt/`), short-circuiting the diff check, mocking the verification call, or otherwise bypassing the math does NOT count as recovery and earns no credit for this goal even if the harness prints PASSED.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline ask in instruction.md: 'write a new script that reconstructs weight given the groundtruth proj_down, proj_up, qweight, wscales, smooth_factor, and verify your result against the groundtruth weight'. The user explicitly names the six target parameters and tells the agent 'for now let's focus on attn.to_out.0'; max_diff < 0.1 is the PASSED bar baked into the workspace's main() verification loop the user hands the agent. The square-shape pair is the first half of the six. Square shapes are the easier subset (N=K=3072, no rectangular tile edge cases) and a partial fix that only handles them lands here.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the reconstruction in reconstruct_weight.py recovers all four rectangular-shape MLP parameters named in the instruction (img_mlp.net.0.proj, img_mlp.net.2, txt_mlp.net.0.proj, txt_mlp.net.2) with max element-wise difference < 0.1 against their ground-truth weight tensors — the same PASSED threshold the workspace harness uses. As with goal_1, the pass must come from genuinely inverting the packed inputs (proj_down/proj_up/qweight/wscales/smooth_factor) per parameter: hardcoding or loading the ground-truth `weight` tensor from `pt/`, returning per-name lookups of the ground truth, branching on parameter name to swap in cached results, or otherwise bypassing the unpack/reconstruct math does NOT count for this goal even if the harness reports PASSED.
completeness_goals.rationale: Coverage requirement explicit in instruction.md: 'test the weight reconstruction using all 6 parameters attn.to_out.0, attn.to_add_out, img_mlp.net.0.proj, img_mlp.net.2, txt_mlp.net.0.proj, txt_mlp.net.2 in the folder pt'. Rectangular shapes (12288x3072 and 3072x12288) exercise non-square code paths — differing group counts along the K axis, asymmetric tile dimensions, and the up-vs-down lowrank projection orientations — that a square-only fix can pass while still being structurally wrong, so they are carved out from goal_1 to penalize patches that hardcode N==K assumptions.

completeness_goals.goal: The fix in reconstruct_weight.py is a principled inversion of the upstream packing format — i.e., the integer-weight bit-packed layout is unpacked (not just byte-split), the per-group scales are unpacked rather than naively transposed, and the low-rank up/down projections are unpacked rather than used in their packed memory layout — so that the end-to-end reconstruction is correct for both square and rectangular shapes rather than for one shape class by accident.
completeness_goals.rationale: Captures the substantive engineering required by the instruction's 'read the original source code in the folders nunchaku and ComfyUI-Nunchaku to understand how the SVDQ layer works' clause and the three packing-format inversions implied by the task (qweight, wscales, lowrank up/down). Folded into a single diagnostic gate because the three sub-stages co-pass when goals 1 and 2 both pass across square and rectangular shapes — splitting them into three independent secondaries (as v2 of this rubric did before consolidation) gave them duplicative weight against the cores. Diagnostic tier means this is informational: a patch that fails this conceptual check but somehow passes both shape classes still gets the cores.

decomposition_notes: Two cores (square shapes, rectangular shapes) at 0.45 each, one diagnostic at 0.10, one anti-effort at 0.00 (sum = 1.00). Square/rectangular split is justified because the failure-mode partition matches: hardcoded-N==K patches pass the cores' square half and fail the rectangular half, and the user explicitly names six parameters across both shape classes, so collapsing them into one core would let a half-correct patch take the full headline weight. Diagnostic gate exists because the three packing-format inversions (qweight bit-packing, per-group scales, lowrank up/down) are the literal source-code reading task asked for in instruction.md, but their pass/fail correlates so tightly with the cores that giving them separate weight would double-count. Anti-effort goal_0 has weight 0.0 by convention; it is a guard predicate, not a credit-bearing line item — if it fails, the other goals are evaluated as if no work was done.

goal_0.user_source: instruction.md (workspace-handover): 'I'm trying to reimplement Nunchaku SVDQ in @quantize_nunchaku_borrow.py' + the workspace's pre-existing reconstruct_weight.py with a reconstruct_weight(name) function name that the verification loop calls
user_verbatim_anchors: write a new script that reconstructs weight
goal_0.anti_gaming_role: Prevents crediting scratch-file work that does not flow through the entrypoint the user's workspace contract uses

user_verbatim_anchors: write a new script that reconstructs weight given the groundtruth proj_down, proj_up, qweight, wscales, smooth_factor, and verify your result against the groundtruth weight
for now let's focus on attn.to_out.0

goal_2.user_source: instruction.md turn 1 (same message that lists all six parameters)
user_verbatim_anchors: attn.to_out.0, attn.to_add_out, img_mlp.net.0.proj, img_mlp.net.2, txt_mlp.net.0.proj, txt_mlp.net.2
read the original source code in the folders nunchaku and ComfyUI-Nunchaku to understand how the SVDQ layer works
reconstructs weight given the groundtruth proj_down, proj_up, qweight, wscales, smooth_factor
goal_3.anti_gaming_role: Diagnostic-only; guards against shape-class-specific accidents but does not double-count the cores

_self_critique_notes: Pass-1 audit checked: (a) every goal cites a user-turn verbatim anchor in _provenance — yes, all four anchor in instruction.md turn 1 (the only user turn the Harbor task surfaces, since multi-turn was collapsed to single-shot per user_simulation_prompt.md); (b) no oracle-specific identifiers — checked, no line numbers, no specific permutation tuples, no diff magnitudes like 0.015, no oracle reward 0.85, no oracle filenames beyond reconstruct_weight.py which IS the user's workspace entrypoint and is independently grounded by the workspace handover (see _dropped_from_v2); (c) anti-gaming clauses inside goals — goal_0 is the explicit anti-scratch-file clause, goal_2 contains anti-N==K-hardcoding guard, goal_3 is anti-shape-class-luck; (d) goal_0 present at weight 0.0; (e) weights independent of F2P (F2P weights are 0.5/0.5 split across two test commands; rubric weights are 0.45/0.45/0.10/0.00 over goal completeness — different decomposition); (f) coverage — every USER ask in instruction.md maps to at least one goal: 'reconstruct weight' -> goals 1,2; 'verify against ground truth' -> goals 1,2 (PASSED threshold); 'all 6 parameters' -> goals 1+2 jointly; 'read source code, understand packing' -> goal 3; 'reimplement in the workspace script' -> goal 0; (g) no goal references a single trial's behavior or model.

_self_critique_log.criticism: PARTIAL on anti_cheating_clauses: 'While goal_3 contains excellent anti-gaming language, the heavily weighted core goals (goal_1 and goal_2) lack explicit anti-gaming clauses inside their own bodies to prevent hardcoded outputs or test-mocking.' Single biggest risk: 'An LLM judge might award full credit for goal_1 and goal_2 if an agent simply hardcodes the ground-truth tensors to pass the tests, since the anti-gaming language is isolated in goal_3.'
_self_critique_log.fix: Appended explicit anti-gaming sentences inside the goal bodies of goal_1 and goal_2 (the two 0.45-weight cores) requiring the PASSED max-diff to come from a genuine inversion of proj_down/proj_up/qweight/wscales/smooth_factor as fed by the harness. Both clauses explicitly deny credit for: loading or hardcoding the ground-truth `weight` tensor from `pt/`, returning per-name lookups of ground truth, branching on parameter name to swap in cached results, short-circuiting the diff check, or mocking the verification call — even if the harness prints PASSED. Goal_3's pre-existing 'principled inversion' diagnostic clause is now reinforced rather than carrying the anti-gaming burden alone. No weight changes; weights still sum to 1.00 (0.0 + 0.45 + 0.45 + 0.10).

_self_critique_log.criticism: anti_effort_floor FAIL: 'goal_0 guards against scratch files but doesn't name standard benchmark-wide anti-effort patterns (git-ops exfiltration, zero-edits, comment-only diffs).' The task-specific scratch-file clause is correct but insufficient — the cross-task anti-effort floor recurring across the benchmark (zero-edits-with-large-patch from sandbox-artifact capture, git checkout/reset/pull exfiltration, comment/whitespace-only diffs, deletion of working tests as a 'fix') needs to be enumerated inside goal_0 so the judge has explicit prose to fall back on rather than inferring it from the scratch-file example alone.
_self_critique_log.fix: Extended goal_0's goal body to enumerate the four standard anti-effort patterns (a)-(d): (a) zero-edits-with-large-patch (captured-tree artifact, sandbox snapshot rather than agent work), (b) git-ops exfiltration (git checkout main, git reset --hard origin/main, git pull, applying downloaded patch files), (c) comment-only / whitespace-only diffs (added comments, docstrings, blank lines, reformatting, import reordering with no executable change), (d) deletion-of-working-tests-as-a-fix (deleting tests, weakening the < 0.1 threshold, short-circuiting assertions, commenting out failing parameters from the verification loop). Existing scratch-file clause retained as the first sentence. Updated rationale to call out that these are the benchmark-wide anti-effort floor. No weight change (goal_0 remains tier=anti_effort, weight=0.0); weights still sum to 1.00.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_goal: goal_3 (scale unpack as a standalone secondary at weight 0.10)
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Folded into v3 goal_3 diagnostic. Splitting scale-unpack, lowrank-unpack, and qweight-unpack into three independent secondaries gave them duplicative weight against the cores: if both cores pass, all three sub-stages must have passed, so they were triple-counting. v2 already noted this in decomposition_notes; v3 makes the fold explicit.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_goal: goal_4 (lowrank unpack as a standalone secondary at weight 0.10)
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Same — collapsed into v3 goal_3 diagnostic.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_goal: goal_5 (qweight unpack as a standalone secondary at weight 0.08)
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Same — collapsed into v3 goal_3 diagnostic. Also v2 rationale cited the specific permutation tuple '(0, 3, 6, 4, 8, 1, 2, 7, 5, 9)' which is an oracle-implementation detail and not a user-pinned identifier; v3 rephrases as 'inverse permutation on the bit-packed integer layout' without naming the tuple.
Single-trial calibration, not part of the task contract. Stripped.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_oracle_identifier: Test.sh internal gate names (SQUARE_OK, RECT_OK, weight_approx)
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Verifier-internal mechanics, not user-visible. v3 says 'PASSED threshold the workspace's verification harness uses' which is the workspace contract the user pins via the pre-existing main() loop.

_added_for_coverage.user_ask_covered: The deliverable is wired through the workspace's reconstruct_weight.py — failure of this trial showed that creating correct-looking unpack code in side files (test_all_correct.py, test_all_unpack.py) without touching reconstruct_weight.py yields a zero reward even when the side files would, in isolation, demonstrate the packing math. The rubric needs an explicit anti-effort clause so a judge does not award partial credit for 'the agent figured out the packing format' when the entrypoint the user is iterating on is unchanged.
_added_for_coverage.v2_status: v2 already had goal_0 as anti-effort at weight 0.0; v3 keeps it and tightens the language to ground in the workspace-handover (the user's instruction message + the workspace state the user gives the agent) rather than only instruction.md prose.

_added_for_coverage.user_ask_covered: instruction.md asks the agent to 'read the original source code in the folders nunchaku and ComfyUI-Nunchaku to understand how the SVDQ layer works in the inference of Qwen-Image-Edit' — i.e., the principled-inversion ask. A judge that only checks the per-parameter diff (goals 1 and 2) cannot distinguish a correct inversion from a shape-class accident where one of the two halves passes for unrelated reasons. Retaining the diagnostic at low weight preserves the signal without double-counting.

_relationship_to_f2p: Rubric weights (0.45 / 0.45 / 0.10 / 0.00) are deliberately not aligned with test_manifest F2P weights (0.5 / 0.5 across the single-param and full-recon test commands). The rubric scores user-ask completeness; F2P scores per-test pass/fail under the workspace's verifier. They are allowed to disagree — e.g., a patch that passes the single-param F2P (attn.to_out.0) but fails the rectangular cases gets ~0.5 from F2P but only ~0.225 from this rubric (half of goal_1 covers attn.to_out.0 alone), which is correct: the user asked for all six, not just the focus parameter.

flags.unpinned_python_deps: Dockerfile pip installs unpinned packages. Reproducibility risk.
